clusters.title: Video: Russia unleashes massive barrage of drones, missiles across Ukraine
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia launches massive overnight attack on Ukraine
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, NPR News and others

items.title: Video: Russia unleashes massive barrage of drones, missiles across Ukraine
items.standfirst: Russia launched a barrage of drones and missiles across Ukraine overnight, killing at least 13 people and injuring dozens more. These videos show the attacks and their aftermath.
items.content: Russia launched a barrage of drones and missiles across Ukraine overnight, killing at least 13 people and injuring dozens more. These videos show the attacks and their aftermath.

items.title: Russia launches massive overnight attack on Ukraine
items.standfirst: Russia launched a massive overnight attack on cities across Ukraine, killing at least 22 people including children.
items.content: Russia launched a massive overnight attack on cities across Ukraine, killing at least 22 people including children.

items.title: Scenes from the aftermath of Russia's latest massive attack on Kyiv
items.standfirst: Russian missile and drone attacks killed civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.
items.content: Russian missile and drone attacks killed civilians across Ukraine overnight, in one of Moscow's largest assaults in recent months.(Image credit: Anton Shtuka for NPR)

items.title: Russian missile, drone attack on Ukraine kills at least 22, officials say
items.standfirst: Russian missile, drone strikes kill at least 22 people across Ukraine, authorities say, after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was planning a "massive new strike."
items.content: Russian missile, drone strikes kill at least 22 people across Ukraine, authorities say, after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was planning a "massive new strike."

items.title: 140,00 Kyiv residents without power after Russian attack
items.standfirst: Russia pounded several Ukrainian cities with hundreds of drones and dozens of ​missiles early on Tuesday, killig18 people and wounding more than 100. Ukraine says Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles overnight. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv.
items.content: Russia pounded several Ukrainian cities with hundreds of drones and dozens of ​missiles early on Tuesday, killig18 people and wounding more than 100. Ukraine says Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles overnight. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv.

items.title: Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine
items.standfirst: At least 18 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian citiesVolodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 18 people and injured dozens more.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, the central cities of Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia, and the eastern cities of Poltava and Kharkiv. Continue reading...
items.content: At least 18 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian citiesVolodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 18 people and injured dozens more.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, the central cities of Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia, and the eastern cities of Poltava and Kharkiv. Continue reading...

items.title: Russia targets Kyiv with massive overnight attack
items.standfirst: More than a dozen people were killed and hundreds more inured after an overnight barrage of Russian missiles and drones targeted the Ukrainian capital. Russia had previously warned that it intended “systematic strikes” on targets in Kyiv linked to Ukraine’s military, urging foreigners to leave.
items.content: More than a dozen people were killed and hundreds more inured after an overnight barrage of Russian missiles and drones targeted the Ukrainian capital. Russia had previously warned that it intended “systematic strikes” on targets in Kyiv linked to Ukraine’s military, urging foreigners to leave.

items.title: Major Russian attack kills at least 22 and leaves Ukraine pleading for help from Trump
items.standfirst: Russian air attacks on major Ukrainian cities such as Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv killed at least nine and wounded more than 60 early Tuesday, authorities said, after days of warnings that Moscow was planning a major assault.
items.content: Russian air attacks on major Ukrainian cities such as Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv killed at least nine and wounded more than 60 early Tuesday, authorities said, after days of warnings that Moscow was planning a major assault.

items.content: Donald Trump appointed a loyalist with no national security experience as head of US intelligence on Tuesday, while allowing him to keep his housing and mortgage oversight roles.

clusters.title: What Trump ‘telling off’ means for Netanyahu
clusters.neutral_headline: What Trump ‘telling off’ means for Netanyahu
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump reportedly lashed out at Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a phone call
Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US

items.title: What Trump ‘telling off’ means for Netanyahu
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump reportedly lashed out at Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a phone call.
items.content: US President Donald Trump reportedly lashed out at Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a phone call.

items.title: Trump's acknowledgment of Hezbollah 'utterly humiliating' for Netanyahu
items.standfirst: Israel continued to strike southern Lebanon on Tuesday as Iran-backed Hezbollah attacked its troops there despite an apparent Washington-brokered de-escalation deal and a fourth round of US-hosted talks between Lebanon and Israel. FRANCE 24's Jerusalem correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky explains that "on the Israeli side we have simply no acknowledgment of this deal", adding that seeing Trump acknowledge Hezbollah "has been an utter humiliation" for Benjamin Netanyahu.
items.content: Israel continued to strike southern Lebanon on Tuesday as Iran-backed Hezbollah attacked its troops there despite an apparent Washington-brokered de-escalation deal and a fourth round of US-hosted talks between Lebanon and Israel. FRANCE 24's Jerusalem correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky explains that "on the Israeli side we have simply no acknowledgment of this deal", adding that seeing Trump acknowledge Hezbollah "has been an utter humiliation" for Benjamin Netanyahu.

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon despite Trump’s effort to shore up ceasefire
items.standfirst: Warplanes carry out dozens of airstrikes and Israeli army issues evacuation warning for city of NabatiyehIsraeli warplanes have launched dozens of strikes across southern Lebanon despite a new agreement supposedly brokered by Donald Trump aiming to bolster the tattered ceasefire in Lebanon.The US president said on Monday that he had stopped an imminent Israeli strike on Beirut and that he had spoken to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both agreed that “all shooting will stop”. Continue reading...
items.content: Warplanes carry out dozens of airstrikes and Israeli army issues evacuation warning for city of NabatiyehIsraeli warplanes have launched dozens of strikes across southern Lebanon despite a new agreement supposedly brokered by Donald Trump aiming to bolster the tattered ceasefire in Lebanon.The US president said on Monday that he had stopped an imminent Israeli strike on Beirut and that he had spoken to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both agreed that “all shooting will stop”. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
items.standfirst: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.
items.content: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.

items.title: Trump ‘shouted and cursed Netanyahu over threat to resume Beirut bombing’
items.standfirst: Angry phone call took place after Iran said it would suspend talks with US over Israel’s Lebanon campaign, Axios reportsDonald Trump angrily confronted Benjamin Netanyahu over Israel’s threats to resume airstrikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, according to a report.“What the fuck are you doing?” the US president shouted at the Israeli prime minister during the phone call on Monday, according to Axios, a US website that has frequently published reports on high-level conversations between the two leaders. Continue reading...
items.content: Angry phone call took place after Iran said it would suspend talks with US over Israel’s Lebanon campaign, Axios reportsDonald Trump angrily confronted Benjamin Netanyahu over Israel’s threats to resume airstrikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, according to a report.“What the fuck are you doing?” the US president shouted at the Israeli prime minister during the phone call on Monday, according to Axios, a US website that has frequently published reports on high-level conversations between the two leaders. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel conducts deadly attacks on southern Lebanon after Trump promises de-escalation
items.standfirst: Israel ​kept up strikes on southern Lebanon on Tuesday, killing eight people, including children, as it pressed its campaign against Hezbollah a day after President Donald Trump asked Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to attack Beirut to avert a further escalation.
items.content: Israel ​kept up strikes on southern Lebanon on Tuesday, killing eight people, including children, as it pressed its campaign against Hezbollah a day after President Donald Trump asked Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu not to attack Beirut to avert a further escalation.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade new attacks despite Trump promise of de-escalation
items.standfirst: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks
items.content: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks

clusters.title: Trump signs AI safety order seeking voluntary review of new models
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump signs AI safety order seeking voluntary review of new models
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, France24, CBS News

items.title: Trump signs AI safety order seeking voluntary review of new models
items.standfirst: The order asks AI companies to voluntarily submit their most powerful models for the government to test up to 30 days before releasing them to the public.
items.content: '/>The order asks AI companies to voluntarily submit their most powerful models for the government to test up to 30 days before releasing them to the public.(Image credit: Graeme Sloan)

items.title: Trump signs order allowing AI companies to give government access to models before release
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order to enable leading AI developers to voluntarily ​submit their most capable models for government cybersecurity tests before releasing them to the public. The order was triggered by concerns over Anthropic's Mythos model, which the company refused to release due to its ability to expose vulnerabilities in computer systems.
items.content: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order to enable leading AI developers to voluntarily ​submit their most capable models for government cybersecurity tests before releasing them to the public. The order was triggered by concerns over Anthropic's Mythos model, which the company refused to release due to its ability to expose vulnerabilities in computer systems.

items.title: Trump signs AI executive order to give government early look at new models
items.standfirst: The order asks AI companies to share previews of powerful new models with the government before they are released to the public.
items.content: The order asks AI companies to share previews of powerful new models with the government before they are released to the public.

items.standfirst: Tommy Robinson attends demonstration outside police station, while others clash with officers near killer’s homeHundreds of people gathered outside a Southampton police station to protest against the murder of Henry Nowak and dozens clashed with police close to the home of his killer, Vickrum Digwa.The far-right activist Tommy Robinson was among speakers who addressed the crowd outside Southampton central police station at the “Justice for Henry Nowak” protest. Continue reading...
items.content: Tommy Robinson attends demonstration outside police station, while others clash with officers near killer’s homeHundreds of people gathered outside a Southampton police station to protest against the murder of Henry Nowak and dozens clashed with police close to the home of his killer, Vickrum Digwa.The far-right activist Tommy Robinson was among speakers who addressed the crowd outside Southampton central police station at the “Justice for Henry Nowak” protest. items.title: 'I can't breathe': Protesters attack police at UK rally over student murder
items.standfirst: Protesters in the southern British city of Southampton on Tuesday attacked police at the site of the murder of an 18-year-old student who was handcuffed as he lay dying in December from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack. Far-right firebrand figure Tommy Robinson addressed the crowd, with many of them waving Union Jack and England flags.
items.content: Protesters in the southern British city of Southampton on Tuesday attacked police at the site of the murder of an 18-year-old student who was handcuffed as he lay dying in December from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack. Far-right firebrand figure Tommy Robinson addressed the crowd, with many of them waving Union Jack and England flags.

clusters.title: ‘Milestone’: Macron opens Paris monument honouring Rwanda genocide victims
clusters.neutral_headline: ‘Milestone’: Macron opens Paris monument honouring Rwanda genocide victims
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24

items.title: ‘Milestone’: Macron opens Paris monument honouring Rwanda genocide victims
items.standfirst: President Macron, acknowledging France's 'responsibility' in the genocide, calls memorial a reconciliation 'milestone'.
items.content: President Macron, acknowledging France's 'responsibility' in the genocide, calls memorial a reconciliation 'milestone'.

items.title: Macron unveils Rwanda genocide memorial in Paris, marking 'quest for truth'
items.standfirst: French President ⁠Emmanuel Macron and ​his Rwandan counterpart Paul Kagame on Tuesday inaugurated a ​memorial in Paris honouring the victims of the 1994 genocide. Unveiling the installation on the banks of the Seine, Macron said the monument marked "the culmination of a long and patient quest for truth" in confronting France's failure to heed warnings of the impending massacres more than 30 years ago.
items.content: French President ⁠Emmanuel Macron and ​his Rwandan counterpart Paul Kagame on Tuesday inaugurated a ​memorial in Paris honouring the victims of the 1994 genocide. Unveiling the installation on the banks of the Seine, Macron said the monument marked "the culmination of a long and patient quest for truth" in confronting France's failure to heed warnings of the impending massacres more than 30 years ago.

clusters.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
clusters.neutral_headline: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, NPR News

items.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
items.standfirst: The European Union has moved forward with a vast overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and ink controversial deals to build detention centers abroad. Rights groups have criticized it, comparing the new regulations to the Trump administration's aggressive immigration policies. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Camille Le Coz, Director of the Migration Policy Institute of Europe, says that the way the policy has been framed in the EU law "is to make it compatible with international law".
items.content: The European Union has moved forward with a vast overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and ink controversial deals to build detention centers abroad. Rights groups have criticized it, comparing the new regulations to the Trump administration's aggressive immigration policies. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Camille Le Coz, Director of the Migration Policy Institute of Europe, says that the way the policy has been framed in the EU law "is to make it compatible with international law".

items.title: EU strikes migration deal for more deportations and detention centers abroad
items.standfirst: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.
items.content: The European Union has moved forward with an overhaul of its migration policy, aiming to ramp up deportations and build detention centers abroad. Critics compared the regulation to the immigration strategy of the Trump administration.(Image credit: Jean-Francois Badias)

items.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
items.standfirst: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps countryMozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others.However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be officially linked to a wave of anti-migrant protests sweeping the country. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
clusters.neutral_headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
items.standfirst: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of TuesdayDenmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition.Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Green Left and the centrist Moderates ended two months of uncertainty after March elections in which 12 parties won seats in parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of TuesdayDenmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition.Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Green Left and the centrist Moderates ended two months of uncertainty after March elections in which 12 parties won seats in parliament. Continue reading...

items.content: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.
